topic: I bought a box of unmarked VHS tapes at an estate sale for two dollars
title: I Bought a Box of Unmarked VHS Tapes at an Estate Sale for Two Dollars | True Scary Story

scenes.narration: I paid two dollars for a box of unmarked VHS tapes. One had footage of my street.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a CRT television screen showing VHS playback of a residential street at night, one house visible with its porch light on, the image grainy with scan lines and slight static, the dim viewing room around the TV in shadow, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Cardboard box. Sixteen tapes. No labels at all.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of an open cardboard box on a folding table at an estate sale, interior filled with stacked VHS cassette tapes, all black with no labels, one tape resting on top with the tape window facing up, flat overcast light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Two dollars. Nobody else wanted them.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of two dollar bills on a folding table next to an open cardboard box of VHS tapes, a hand reaching into frame to take the money, household items visible in soft focus behind, flat daylight, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: The first tape was home footage. A kitchen. A family I did not know.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a CRT television screen showing VHS playback of a 1980s kitchen interior, two adults visible from behind at a kitchen counter, the image in the washed-out color of home video, scan lines across the screen, dim viewing room around the TV, vertical 9:16 framing

close shot of a hand holding a black VHS cassette, the tape housing slightly warped on one corner, no label, just the number nine written in blue ballpoint pen on the spine, ambient living room light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Static. Then a hallway. Then nothing moved for forty minutes.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a CRT television showing VHS footage of an empty residential hallway, overhead light on, closed doors at both ends, the image slightly overexposed, scan lines and mild static, the hallway completely still, dark room around the TV, vertical 9:16 framing

extreme close up of a hand pressing the fast-forward button on a VHS player, the button worn smooth from use, the VCR deck visible below with a green time display, dim ambient living room light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: At minute forty one. My street. My house.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a CRT television screen showing VHS footage of a residential street at night, one house visible with its porch light on, the image grainy and slightly dark in the way of home video shot at night, scan lines, the scene paused mid-frame, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: I checked the date stamp. Recorded last October.
scenes.image_prompt: extreme close up of the corner of a CRT television screen showing a VHS date and time stamp in blocky white digits, the month and year visible, the rest of the screen blurred in the background, dim ambient light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: I had moved into that house in November. Someone recorded my street before I lived there and put the tape in a box for a stranger.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot of a living room at night, a CRT television on a stand with a paused VHS image glowing on screen showing a dark residential street, a person sitting on a couch facing the TV with their back to the camera, the rest of the room dark, vertical 9:16 framing

close shot of an open black garbage bin with a cardboard box of VHS tapes dropped inside, one tape visible resting on top with the tape window facing up, exterior dim light from a porch lamp, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: The bin was empty the next morning. All sixteen tapes gone.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of an open empty plastic garbage bin at the side of a house, the interior clean and bare, morning light flat and grey, a crack in the driveway running beside the bin, no other objects in frame, vertical 9:16 framing
